title: Correlation and partial correlation tables
text: tbl_correlation() formats tidy data frame (partial) correlation results publication-ready table. expects pre-computed coefficients tidy data frame one row per predictor x outcome pair. Column-name arguments take quoted strings. Defaults match typical correlation results frame columns named variable, outcome, r, p:
code: head(clerk_cor_example) #>      variable  outcome   n      r     p #> 1         hdl tmt_time 270  0.190 0.008 #> 2     glucose tmt_time 254 -0.229 0.398 #> 3         bmi tmt_time 290 -0.086 0.013 #> 4       waist tmt_time 253  0.279 0.002 #> 5 systolic_bp tmt_time 242  0.195 0.379 #> 6         bdi tmt_time 281  0.096 0.109

title: Correlation and partial correlation tables
text: Since clerk_cor_example already matches defaults, minimal call suffices:
code: tbl_correlation(   clerk_cor_example,   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Partial correlations (age + sex controlled)")

title: Correlation and partial correlation tables
text: Pass column name string n:
code: tbl_correlation(   clerk_cor_example,   n      = "n",   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Partial correlations (age + sex controlled)")

title: Correlation and partial correlation tables
code: tbl_correlation(   clerk_cor_example,   n       = "n",   domains = list(     "Metabolic"      = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Anthropometric" = c("waist", "systolic_bp"),     "Mental health"  = c("bdi", "panas_neg", "life_satisfaction")   ),   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Partial correlations (age + sex controlled)") #> Warning in `[<-.factor`(`*tmp*`, is.na(tbl[["domain"]]), value = "Other"): #> invalid factor level, NA generated

title: Correlation and partial correlation tables
text: Set fdr = TRUE add BH-corrected p_fdr column. Use fdr_within correct separately within outcome:
code: tbl_correlation(   clerk_cor_example,   fdr        = TRUE,   fdr_within = "outcome",   output     = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(     title    = "Partial correlations (age + sex controlled)",     footnote = "FDR correction applied within each outcome (BH)."   )

title: Correlation and partial correlation tables
text: Set pivot = TRUE show one column per outcome, r (p) values cell:
code: tbl_correlation(   clerk_cor_example,   domains = list(     "Metabolic"    = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Mental health"= c("bdi", "panas_neg")   ),   fdr    = TRUE,   pivot  = TRUE,   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(     title    = "Partial correlations (age + sex controlled)",     footnote = "Values shown as r (p FDR). FDR-corrected p-value (BH)."   ) #> Warning in `[<-.factor`(`*tmp*`, is.na(tbl[["domain"]]), value = "Other"): #> invalid factor level, NA generated

title: Correlation and partial correlation tables
text: results frame uses different column names, pass strings:
code: my_results <- data.frame(   predictor_var = c("bmi", "hdl"),   outcome_var   = c("tmt_time", "tmt_time"),   rho           = c(0.12, -0.08),   pval          = c(0.021, 0.140),   sample_n      = c(249, 249) )  tbl_correlation(   my_results,   predictor = "predictor_var",   outcome   = "outcome_var",   r         = "rho",   p         = "pval",   n         = "sample_n",   fdr       = TRUE,   output    = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Partial correlations (age + sex controlled)")

title: Correlation and partial correlation tables
code: tbl_correlation(   clerk_cor_example,   domains = list(     "Metabolic"    = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Mental health"= c("bdi", "panas_neg")   ),   fdr    = TRUE,   output = "html" ) |>   clerk_render() #> Warning in `[<-.factor`(`*tmp*`, is.na(tbl[["domain"]]), value = "Other"): #> invalid factor level, NA generated

title: Descriptive tables (Table 1)
text: tbl_descriptive() produces Table 1-style sample characteristics summary. Continuous variables shown mean ± SD; categorical variables n (%). Group comparisons use independent-samples t-tests (two groups) one-way ANOVA (three groups) continuous variables chi-squared tests categorical variables.

title: Descriptive tables (Table 1)
text: minimum call needs data frame. Without group argument get overall summary column.
code: tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Sample characteristics")

title: Descriptive tables (Table 1)
text: Pass unquoted column name group add per-group columns statistical test.
code: tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group  = sex,   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Sample characteristics by sex")

title: Descriptive tables (Table 1)
text: Use domains argument organise rows labelled section headers. variable mentioned domains placed “”.
code: tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group   = sex,   domains = list(     "Metabolic"      = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Anthropometric" = c("waist", "systolic_bp"),     "Cognitive"      = c("tmt_time", "verbal_fluency"),     "Mental health"  = c("bdi", "panas_neg", "life_satisfaction")   ),   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Table 1. Sample characteristics by sex") #> Warning in `[<-.factor`(`*tmp*`, is.na(tbl[["domain"]]), value = "Other"): #> invalid factor level, NA generated

title: Descriptive tables (Table 1)
text: Use log_vars flag variables log-transformed analysis shown raw scale. footnote added automatically.
code: tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group    = sex,   domains  = list(     "Cognitive" = c("tmt_time", "verbal_fluency")   ),   log_vars = "tmt_time",   output   = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(     title    = "Cognitive variables by sex",     footnote = "Continuous variables: mean \u00b1 SD. Group comparisons: t-test."   ) #> Warning in `[<-.factor`(`*tmp*`, is.na(tbl[["domain"]]), value = "Other"): #> invalid factor level, NA generated

title: Descriptive tables (Table 1)
text: Set fdr = TRUE apply Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction across p-values. p_fdr column added table.
code: tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group  = sex,   fdr    = TRUE,   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Sample characteristics by sex (FDR-corrected)")

title: Descriptive tables (Table 1)
text: Use vars dplyr selection helpers include subset columns.
code: tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group  = sex,   vars   = dplyr::any_of(c("age", "hdl", "glucose", "bmi")),   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Metabolic variables by sex")

title: Descriptive tables (Table 1)
text: Set output = "html" interactive searchable table.
code: tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group  = sex,   output = "html" ) |>   clerk_render()

title: Descriptive tables (Table 1)
text: clerk_tbl object plain list. Access $table see formatted data frame rendering.

title: Getting started with clerkR
text: clerkR turns standard R data frames publication-ready tables biomedical neuroscience manuscripts. covers five table archetypes together account large majority results tables typical paper: Every function follows two-step pattern: Construct — call tbl_*() function build clerk_tbl object. Render — pipe clerk_render() produce output format need: gt Word/PDF, html interactive table, latex manuscript compiled LaTeX engine. output format set construction time via output argument travels object, clerk_render() always knows produce.
code: tbl_*(data, ..., output = "gt") |>   clerk_render(title = "My table")

title: Getting started with clerkR
code: remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/clerkR")

title: Getting started with clerkR
text: clerkR ships four synthetic datasets — values simulated bear relation real study.
code: library(clerkR) # 300 participants, demographic + metabolic + cognitive + mental health head(clerk_example, 3) #>      sex  age   hdl glucose  bmi waist systolic_bp tmt_time verbal_fluency bdi #> 1   Male 46.4 29.96    55.0 26.6  87.1         130    140.2             12  24 #> 2   Male 23.7 40.49    47.2 27.6  90.3         102     95.5             22   0 #> 3 Female 64.5 51.71    84.2 26.5 100.6         117    122.1             17   6 #>   panas_neg life_satisfaction #> 1         7                25 #> 2        29                16 #> 3        19                25  # 16 predictor × outcome partial correlation results head(clerk_cor_example, 3) #>   variable  outcome   n      r     p #> 1      hdl tmt_time 270  0.190 0.008 #> 2  glucose tmt_time 254 -0.229 0.398 #> 3      bmi tmt_time 290 -0.086 0.013  # 7 regression terms (broom::tidy() format) clerk_reg_example #>          term estimate std.error conf.low conf.high p.value #> 1         bmi    0.021     0.023   -0.024     0.066   0.012 #> 2       waist   -0.018     0.024   -0.065     0.029   0.048 #> 3 systolic_bp    0.012     0.011   -0.010     0.034   0.210 #> 4         bdi    0.008     0.022   -0.035     0.051   0.330 #> 5   panas_neg    0.015     0.018   -0.020     0.050   0.041 #> 6         age    0.003     0.015   -0.026     0.032   0.610 #> 7     sexMale   -0.045     0.020   -0.084    -0.006   0.003  # 12 heritability estimates (herit_batch() format) head(clerk_h2_example, 3) #>           trait covariates   h2 ci_lo ci_hi  pval sigma2_a sigma2_e #> 1   K (tension)      unadj 0.48  0.28  0.70 5e-04    0.312    0.338 #> 2     S (shape)      unadj 0.73  0.57  0.88 8e-04    0.474    0.176 #> 3 I (isometric)      unadj 0.45  0.27  0.64 9e-04    0.293    0.358

title: Getting started with clerkR
code: tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group  = sex,   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Table 1. Sample characteristics by sex")

title: Getting started with clerkR
text: Group rows section labels domains argument — named list mapping variable names section headers.
code: tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group   = sex,   domains = list(     "Metabolic"      = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Anthropometric" = c("waist", "systolic_bp"),     "Cognitive"      = c("tmt_time", "verbal_fluency"),     "Mental health"  = c("bdi", "panas_neg", "life_satisfaction")   ),   log_vars = "tmt_time",   fdr      = TRUE,   output   = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Table 1. Sample characteristics by sex") #> Warning in `[<-.factor`(`*tmp*`, is.na(tbl[["domain"]]), value = "Other"): #> invalid factor level, NA generated

title: Getting started with clerkR
text: Swap output = "gt" output = "html" get interactive reactable table, output = "latex" LaTeX source.
code: tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group  = sex,   output = "html" ) |>   clerk_render()

title: Getting started with clerkR
text: tables styled clerkR colour palette automatically via clerk_theme(). can also access palette directly use figures:
code: clerk_palette() #>         navy     mid_blue   light_teal   near_white   terracotta    header_bg  #>    "#293681"    "#4274D9"    "#95CCDD"    "#D0E7E6"    "#D4907E"    "#95CCDD"  #>  header_text row_group_bg    body_text     positive     negative      neutral  #>    "#293681"    "#D0E7E6"    "#293681"    "#293681"    "#D4907E"    "#F0EEEC" clerk_diverging(n = 9)   # terracotta → off-white → navy #> [1] "#D4907E" "#DBA799" "#E2BFB5" "#E9D6D0" "#F0EEEC" "#BEC0D1" "#8C92B6" #> [8] "#5A649B" "#293681" clerk_sequential(n = 7)  # near-white → navy #> [1] "#D0E7E6" "#B2D9E1" "#95CCDD" "#6BA0DB" "#4274D9" "#3555AD" "#293681"

title: Getting started with clerkR
text: archetype vignette full worked example: vignette("descriptive-table1") — Table 1 group comparisons vignette("simple-descriptive") — Summary table without tests vignette("correlation") — Partial correlation results vignette("regression") — Regression coefficient tables vignette("heritability-ritable") — Heritability R-itable

title: Heritability tables with R-itable and clerkR
text: clerkR::tbl_heritability() designed accept output R-itable::herit_batch() directly. Column-name defaults match herit_batch() output exactly: covariate model column "covariates" herit_batch() output — pass model = "covariates" include .

title: Heritability tables with R-itable and clerkR
text: Install packages GitHub haven’t already:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/R-itable") remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/clerkR") library(Ritable) library(clerkR)

title: Heritability tables with R-itable and clerkR
code: set.seed(2026) n_fam <- 80  ped <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(n_fam), function(f) {   base <- (f - 1L) * 5L   data.frame(     id  = base + 1:5,     pat = c(0L, 0L, base + 1L, base + 1L, base + 1L),     mom = c(0L, 0L, base + 2L, base + 2L, base + 2L),     sex = c(1L, 2L, sample(1:2, 3, replace = TRUE))   ) }))  simulate_trait <- function(ped, n_fam, h2, seed) {   set.seed(seed)   genetic  <- rep(rnorm(n_fam, 0, sqrt(h2)),      each = 5)   residual <- rnorm(nrow(ped),         0, sqrt(1 - h2))   genetic + residual }  pheno_all <- data.frame(   bmi = simulate_trait(ped, n_fam, h2 = 0.60, seed = 1),   sbp = simulate_trait(ped, n_fam, h2 = 0.35, seed = 2),   hdl = simulate_trait(ped, n_fam, h2 = 0.50, seed = 3) )  off_rows <- ped$pat != 0L dat <- data.frame(   IID     = ped$id[off_rows],   age     = round(runif(sum(off_rows), 20, 75)),   sex_num = ped$sex[off_rows],   bmi     = pheno_all$bmi[off_rows],   sbp     = pheno_all$sbp[off_rows],   hdl     = pheno_all$hdl[off_rows] ) dat$age2 <- dat$age^2

what: 2. Build GRM and run batch estimation
title: Heritability tables with R-itable and clerkR
code: A <- build_grm(ped, study_ids = dat$IID)  res <- herit_batch(   traits    = c("bmi", "sbp", "hdl"),   grm       = A,   data      = dat,   covs_list = list(     unadj = NULL,     cov1  = c("age", "sex_num"),     cov2  = c("age", "sex_num", "age2")   ) )  res

title: Heritability tables with R-itable and clerkR
text: Pass model = "covariates" optional variance component column names strings:
code: res |>   tbl_heritability(     model    = "covariates",     sigma2_a = "sigma2_a",     sigma2_e = "sigma2_e",     domains  = list("Cardiometabolic" = c("bmi", "sbp", "hdl")),     fdr      = TRUE,     output   = "gt"   ) |>   clerk_render(     title    = "Heritability estimates",     subtitle = "Narrow-sense h\u00b2 \u00b1 95% profile-likelihood CI",     footnote = "FDR correction applied within each covariate model (BH). p-values are one-sided LRT with chi-squared(1) boundary correction."   )

title: Heritability tables with R-itable and clerkR
code: res |>   subset(covariates == "cov2") |>   tbl_heritability(     sigma2_a = "sigma2_a",     sigma2_e = "sigma2_e",     fdr      = TRUE,     output   = "gt"   ) |>   clerk_render(     title    = "Heritability estimates (age + sex + age\u00b2)",     footnote = "FDR correction applied across all traits (BH)."   )

title: Heritability tables with R-itable and clerkR
code: res |>   subset(covariates == "cov2") |>   tbl_heritability(     sigma2_a = "sigma2_a",     sigma2_e = "sigma2_e",     output   = "html"   ) |>   clerk_render()

title: Regression coefficient tables
text: tbl_regression() formats tidy data frame regression results — β, SE, 95% CI, p-value — publication-ready table. designed accept output broom::tidy() directly. Column-name arguments take quoted strings. Defaults match broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) output: clerk_reg_example already uses column names:

title: Regression coefficient tables
text: Since clerk_reg_example matches defaults, remapping needed:
code: tbl_regression(   clerk_reg_example,   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Linear regression: TMT completion time (log s)")

title: Regression coefficient tables
code: tbl_regression(   clerk_reg_example,   domains = list(     "Cardiometabolic" = c("bmi", "waist", "systolic_bp"),     "Mental health"   = c("bdi", "panas_neg")   ),   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Linear regression: TMT completion time (log s)") #> Warning in `[<-.factor`(`*tmp*`, is.na(tbl[["domain"]]), value = "Other"): #> invalid factor level, NA generated

title: Regression coefficient tables
code: tbl_regression(   clerk_reg_example,   domains = list(     "Cardiometabolic" = c("bmi", "waist", "systolic_bp"),     "Mental health"   = c("bdi", "panas_neg")   ),   fdr    = TRUE,   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(     title    = "Linear regression: TMT completion time (log s)",     footnote = "FDR correction applied across all terms (BH)."   ) #> Warning in `[<-.factor`(`*tmp*`, is.na(tbl[["domain"]]), value = "Other"): #> invalid factor level, NA generated

title: Regression coefficient tables
code: library(broom)  lm(log(tmt_time) ~ bmi + waist + systolic_bp + bdi + panas_neg + age + sex,    data = clerk_example) |>   tidy(conf.int = TRUE) |>   tbl_regression(     domains = list(       "Cardiometabolic" = c("bmi", "waist", "systolic_bp"),       "Mental health"   = c("bdi", "panas_neg")     ),     fdr    = TRUE,     output = "gt"   ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Linear regression: TMT completion time (log s)")

title: Regression coefficient tables
text: Set exponentiate = TRUE show odds ratios hazard ratios:
code: library(broom)  glm(outcome ~ bmi + age + sex, data = my_data, family = binomial) |>   tidy(conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = FALSE) |>   tbl_regression(     exponentiate = TRUE,     output       = "gt"   ) |>   clerk_render(     title    = "Logistic regression: odds ratios",     footnote = "Values shown as OR [95% CI]."   )

title: Regression coefficient tables
text: Pass column names strings differ defaults:
code: my_results <- data.frame(   predictor = c("bmi", "age"),   beta      = c(0.021, 0.003),   se        = c(0.008, 0.002),   lo95      = c(0.005, -0.001),   hi95      = c(0.037,  0.007),   pval      = c(0.012,  0.610) )  tbl_regression(   my_results,   term      = "predictor",   estimate  = "beta",   std_error = "se",   conf_low  = "lo95",   conf_high = "hi95",   p         = "pval",   output    = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Regression results")

title: Regression coefficient tables
code: tbl_regression(   clerk_reg_example,   output = "html" ) |>   clerk_render()

title: Simple descriptive tables
text: tbl_simple() produces concise descriptive summary — mean ± SD continuous variables n (%) categorical variables — without group comparisons statistical tests. right choice supplementary tables, sub-cohort descriptions, context need clean summary without inference.

title: Simple descriptive tables
code: tbl_simple(   clerk_example,   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Sample characteristics")

title: Simple descriptive tables
code: tbl_simple(   clerk_example,   vars   = c(age, hdl, glucose, bmi, waist),   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Metabolic and anthropometric characteristics")

title: Simple descriptive tables
code: tbl_simple(   clerk_example,   domains = list(     "Metabolic"      = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Anthropometric" = c("waist", "systolic_bp"),     "Cognitive"      = c("tmt_time", "verbal_fluency"),     "Mental health"  = c("bdi", "panas_neg", "life_satisfaction")   ),   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Sample characteristics") #> Warning in `[<-.factor`(`*tmp*`, is.na(tbl[["domain"]]), value = "Other"): #> invalid factor level, NA generated

title: Simple descriptive tables
code: tbl_simple(   clerk_example,   domains  = list("Cognitive" = c("tmt_time", "verbal_fluency")),   log_vars = "tmt_time",   output   = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(     title    = "Cognitive variables",     footnote = "Values shown on raw scale."   ) #> Warning in `[<-.factor`(`*tmp*`, is.na(tbl[["domain"]]), value = "Other"): #> invalid factor level, NA generated

title: Simple descriptive tables
code: tbl_simple(   clerk_example,   domains = list(     "Metabolic"    = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Mental health"= c("bdi", "panas_neg")   ),   output = "html" ) |>   clerk_render() #> Warning in `[<-.factor`(`*tmp*`, is.na(tbl[["domain"]]), value = "Other"): #> invalid factor level, NA generated

what: When to use tbl_simple() vs tbl_descriptive()
title: Simple descriptive tables
text: Use tbl_simple() meaningful group comparison — example single-arm cohort, subset table, supplementary overview. Use tbl_descriptive() comparing two groups want statistical test columns alongside summaries.

text: Lucas G. S. Franca. Author, maintainer.

code: @Manual{,   title = {clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research},   author = {Lucas G. S. Franca},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk}, }

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
text: clerk keeps tabs — clerkR keeps publication-ready.

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
text: clerkR transforms standard R data frames publication-ready tables biomedical neuroscience manuscripts. Rather wrestling formatting time, clerkR reduces common table types handful opinionated constructor functions share consistent theming, domain grouping, footnote handling, unified rendering pipeline Word/PDF (gt), interactive HTML (reactable), LaTeX output.

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
text: 📋 Five table archetypes covering ~90% appears biomed paper 🎨 clerkR theme — light teal headers, navy text, clean borders, consistent throughout 🗂️ Domain/section grouping — organise rows labelled section headers 📝 Automatic footnotes log-transformed variables FDR correction 🖨️ Three render targets — gt Word/PDF, reactable HTML, LaTeX manuscripts 🔢 Output baked construction — set output = "gt" , just |> clerk_render() 🔗 R-itable compatible — herit_batch() output pipes straight tbl_heritability() 🧩 Composable — constructors return clerk_tbl S3 object

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
code: clerkR/ ├── R/ │   ├── clerkR-package.R │   ├── tbl_descriptive.R │   ├── tbl_simple.R │   ├── tbl_correlation.R │   ├── tbl_regression.R │   ├── tbl_heritability.R │   ├── render.R               # clerk_render(), render_gt(), render_reactable(), render_latex() │   └── theme.R                # clerk_theme(), clerk_palette(), clerk_colour(), clerk_diverging() ├── data/                      # Synthetic example datasets ├── data-raw/                  # Scripts to generate example data ├── vignettes/                 # Workflow vignettes ├── tests/testthat/ ├── DESCRIPTION └── NAMESPACE

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
code: # clerkR remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/clerkR")  # For the heritability workflow, also install R-itable remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/R-itable")

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
text: Every clerkR workflow follows pattern: construct → render.
code: library(clerkR)  tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group   = sex,   domains = list(     "Metabolic"      = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Anthropometric" = c("waist", "systolic_bp"),     "Cognitive"      = c("tmt_time", "verbal_fluency"),     "Mental health"  = c("bdi", "panas_neg", "life_satisfaction")   ),   log_vars = "tmt_time",   fdr      = TRUE,   output   = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Table 1. Sample characteristics by sex")

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
code: tbl_correlation(   clerk_cor_example,   domains = list(     "Metabolic"    = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Mental health"= c("bdi", "panas_neg")   ),   fdr    = TRUE,   output = "gt" ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Partial correlations (age + sex controlled)")

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
code: # herit_batch() output pipes straight in — no renaming needed herit_batch(traits, grm = A, data = dat, covs_list = covs_list) |>   tbl_heritability(     model    = "covariates",     sigma2_a = "sigma2_a",     sigma2_e = "sigma2_e",     fdr      = TRUE,     output   = "gt"   ) |>   clerk_render(title = "Heritability estimates")

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
code: clerk_palette()     # full named palette clerk_diverging()   # terracotta → off-white → navy (9 steps) clerk_sequential()  # near-white → navy (7 steps)

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
text: 🕐 zeitR — actigraphy analysis circadian metrics 📓 slumbR — sleep diary processing 📋 tallieR — questionnaire sociodemographic data 🔗 syncR — integrates zeitR, slumbR, tallieR 🧬 R-itable — pedigree-based heritability estimation 🔬 circadia-bio — Circadia Lab GitHub organisation

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R

what: clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research — clerkR-package
title: clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research — clerkR-package
text: clerkR transforms standard R data frames publication-ready tables across handful common archetypes found biomedical neuroscience manuscripts.

title: clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research — clerkR-package
text: Descriptive / Table 1 (tbl_descriptive()): sample characteristics group comparisons, mean ± SD, t-test / chi-squared statistics. Heritability (tbl_heritability()): h2, confidence intervals, LRT p, variance components. Directly compatible R-itable::herit_batch(). Correlation (tbl_correlation()): r, p, FDR-corrected p, domain grouping. Regression (tbl_regression()): beta, SE, CI, p, FDR annotation. Simple descriptive (tbl_simple()): means/SDs, inferential test.

title: clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research — clerkR-package
text: constructors return clerk_tbl S3 object. Pass clerk_render() dispatch gt (Word/PDF), reactable (HTML), LaTeX output.

title: clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research — clerkR-package
text: Maintainer: Lucas G. S. Franca lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Authors: Lucas G. S. Franca lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID)

what: Access a single clerkR colour by name — clerk_colour
title: Access a single clerkR colour by name — clerk_colour
text: Convenience accessor single named colour clerkR palette.

title: Access a single clerkR colour by name — clerk_colour
code: clerk_colour(name)

title: Access a single clerkR colour by name — clerk_colour
text: name Character string. One named entries clerk_palette().

title: Access a single clerkR colour by name — clerk_colour
text: single hex colour string.

title: Access a single clerkR colour by name — clerk_colour
code: clerk_colour("navy") #> [1] "#293681" clerk_colour("header_bg") #> [1] "#95CCDD"

what: Synthetic partial correlation results for clerkR examples — clerk_cor_example
title: Synthetic partial correlation results for clerkR examples — clerk_cor_example
text: synthetic dataset partial correlation results (age + sex controlled) eight predictor variables two cognitive outcomes, designed illustrate tbl_correlation(). values simulated bear relation real study.

title: Synthetic partial correlation results for clerkR examples — clerk_cor_example
code: clerk_cor_example

title: Synthetic partial correlation results for clerkR examples — clerk_cor_example
text: data frame 16 rows 5 variables: variable Predictor variable name (character). outcome Outcome variable name: "tmt_time" "verbal_fluency" (character). n Sample size correlation (integer). r Partial correlation coefficient (numeric). p Two-tailed p-value (numeric).

title: Synthetic partial correlation results for clerkR examples — clerk_cor_example
text: Simulated data generated data-raw/clerk_cor_example.R.

what: Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging
title: Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging
text: Returns vector n hex colours interpolated along clerkR diverging scale: dusty terracotta (#D4907E) — neutral -white (#F0EEEC) — navy (#293681). Suitable use gt::data_color() scales::col_numeric().

title: Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging
code: clerk_diverging(n = 9, reverse = FALSE)

title: Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging
text: n Integer. Number colour steps (default 9). Use odd number include neutral midpoint. reverse Logical. Reverse scale direction (default FALSE, terracotta = low, navy = high).

title: Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging
text: character vector n hex colour codes.

title: Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging

what: Synthetic example dataset for clerkR — clerk_example
title: Synthetic example dataset for clerkR — clerk_example
text: synthetic dataset 300 participants demographic, metabolic, anthropometric, cognitive, mental health variables, designed illustrate tbl_descriptive() clerkR table constructors. values simulated bear relation real study.

title: Synthetic example dataset for clerkR — clerk_example
code: clerk_example

title: Synthetic example dataset for clerkR — clerk_example
text: data frame 300 rows 12 variables: sex Factor: "Female" / "Male". age Age years (numeric). hdl HDL cholesterol mmol/L (numeric). glucose Fasting glucose mmol/L (numeric). bmi Body mass index kg/m² (numeric). waist Waist circumference cm (numeric). systolic_bp Systolic blood pressure mmHg (numeric). tmt_time Trail Making Test completion time seconds, log-scale analysis recommended (numeric). verbal_fluency Verbal fluency score — number words 60 s (numeric). bdi Beck Depression Inventory total score (numeric). panas_neg PANAS negative affect subscale score (numeric). life_satisfaction Life Satisfaction Scale total score (numeric).

title: Synthetic example dataset for clerkR — clerk_example
text: Simulated data generated data-raw/clerk_example.R.

what: Synthetic heritability estimates for clerkR examples — clerk_h2_example
title: Synthetic heritability estimates for clerkR examples — clerk_h2_example
text: synthetic data frame narrow-sense heritability (h²) estimates three cortical shape metrics across four covariate models, designed illustrate tbl_heritability(). Column names match output R-itable::herit_batch() direct compatibility. values simulated.

title: Synthetic heritability estimates for clerkR examples — clerk_h2_example
code: clerk_h2_example

title: Synthetic heritability estimates for clerkR examples — clerk_h2_example
text: data frame 12 rows 7 variables: trait Phenotype name (character). covariates Covariate model label (character). h2 Narrow-sense heritability point estimate (numeric). ci_lo Lower 95% profile-likelihood CI bound (numeric). ci_hi Upper 95% profile-likelihood CI bound (numeric). pval One-sided LRT p-value (numeric). sigma2_a Additive genetic variance component (numeric). sigma2_e Residual variance component (numeric).

title: Synthetic heritability estimates for clerkR examples — clerk_h2_example
text: Simulated data generated data-raw/clerk_h2_example.R.

title: clerkR colour palette — clerk_palette
text: Returns named clerkR colour palette character vector hex codes. Individual colours can accessed name via clerk_colour(). palette pairs formal cool-blue range (navy → light teal → near-white) dusty terracotta warm pole diverging scales.

title: clerkR colour palette — clerk_palette
code: clerk_palette()

title: clerkR colour palette — clerk_palette
text: named character vector hex codes.

title: clerkR colour palette — clerk_palette
code: clerk_palette() #>         navy     mid_blue   light_teal   near_white   terracotta    header_bg  #>    "#293681"    "#4274D9"    "#95CCDD"    "#D0E7E6"    "#D4907E"    "#95CCDD"  #>  header_text row_group_bg    body_text     positive     negative      neutral  #>    "#293681"    "#D0E7E6"    "#293681"    "#293681"    "#D4907E"    "#F0EEEC"  clerk_palette()[["header_bg"]] #> [1] "#95CCDD"

what: Synthetic regression results for clerkR examples — clerk_reg_example
title: Synthetic regression results for clerkR examples — clerk_reg_example
text: synthetic data frame linear regression results (outcome: log TMT completion time) designed illustrate tbl_regression(). Mimics output broom::tidy(lm(...), conf.int = TRUE). values simulated.

title: Synthetic regression results for clerkR examples — clerk_reg_example
code: clerk_reg_example

title: Synthetic regression results for clerkR examples — clerk_reg_example
text: data frame 7 rows 6 variables: term Model term / predictor name (character). estimate Regression coefficient β (numeric). std.error Standard error estimate (numeric). conf.low Lower 95% confidence interval bound (numeric). conf.high Upper 95% confidence interval bound (numeric). p.value Two-tailed p-value (numeric).

title: Synthetic regression results for clerkR examples — clerk_reg_example
text: Simulated data generated data-raw/clerk_reg_example.R.

what: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — clerk_render
title: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — clerk_render
text: Dispatches correct renderer based output slot set construction time (tbl_descriptive(..., output = "gt"|"html"|"latex")). render arguments (title, subtitle, footnote) can supplied forwarded underlying renderer.

title: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — clerk_render
code: clerk_render(x, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, footnote = NULL, ...)

title: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — clerk_render
text: x clerk_tbl object. title Optional character string table title. subtitle Optional character string table subtitle. footnote Optional additional footnote text. ... Passed underlying render_gt(), render_reactable(), render_latex().

title: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — clerk_render
text: gt_tbl, reactable, knit_asis object depending output slot x.

title: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — clerk_render

what: Build a clerkR sequential colour scale — clerk_sequential
title: Build a clerkR sequential colour scale — clerk_sequential
text: Returns vector n hex colours interpolated along clerkR sequential scale: near-white (#D0E7E6) → navy (#293681). Suitable shading columns magnitude (e.g. heritability h², correlation r).

title: Build a clerkR sequential colour scale — clerk_sequential
code: clerk_sequential(n = 7, reverse = FALSE)

title: Build a clerkR sequential colour scale — clerk_sequential
text: n Integer. Number colour steps (default 7). reverse Logical. Reverse scale direction (default FALSE, light = low, dark = high).

title: Build a clerkR sequential colour scale — clerk_sequential
text: character vector n hex colour codes.

title: Build a clerkR sequential colour scale — clerk_sequential
code: clerk_sequential() #> [1] "#D0E7E6" "#B2D9E1" "#95CCDD" "#6BA0DB" "#4274D9" "#3555AD" "#293681" clerk_sequential(n = 5, reverse = TRUE) #> [1] "#293681" "#3B64C3" "#6BA0DB" "#A3D2DF" "#D0E7E6"

what: Apply the clerkR gt theme to a gt table — clerk_theme
title: Apply the clerkR gt theme to a gt table — clerk_theme
text: Applies clerkR visual style existing gt_tbl object: light teal column headers navy text, near-white row group bars, clean borders, consistent typography. Can applied gt pipeline. function called automatically render_gt() render_latex(); need directly building gt table outside tbl_* constructors.

title: Apply the clerkR gt theme to a gt table — clerk_theme
code: clerk_theme(gt_tbl)

title: Apply the clerkR gt theme to a gt table — clerk_theme
text: gt_tbl gt_tbl object.

title: Apply the clerkR gt theme to a gt table — clerk_theme
text: gt_tbl object clerkR styling applied.

title: Apply the clerkR gt theme to a gt table — clerk_theme
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gt::gt(mtcars) |> clerk_theme() } # }

what: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt
title: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt
text: Renders clerk_tbl gt table clerkR styling applied via clerk_theme(). Domain groupings become row-group labels; log-transformed variables receive automatic footnote. Typically called indirectly via clerk_render().

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt
code: render_gt(x, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, footnote = NULL, ...)

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt
text: x clerk_tbl object. title Optional table title. subtitle Optional table subtitle. footnote Optional additional footnote. ... Reserved future use.

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt
text: gt_tbl object.

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt

what: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
title: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
text: Renders clerk_tbl LaTeX table via gt::as_latex(). Typically called indirectly via clerk_render().

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
code: render_latex(x, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, footnote = NULL, ...)

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
text: x clerk_tbl object. title Optional table title (used \caption{}). subtitle Optional subtitle appended caption. footnote Optional additional footnote. ... Reserved future use.

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
text: knit_asis character object containing LaTeX table source.

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
code:
tbl_descriptive(clerk_example, group = sex, output = "latex") |>   clerk_render(title = "Sample characteristics by sex") #> [1] "\begin{table}[t]
\caption*{
{\fontsize{10}{12}\selectfont  Sample characteristics by sex\fontsize{9}{11}\selectfont }
} 
\fontsize{9.0pt}{11.0pt}\selectfont

\toprule
 & {\bfseries \cellcolor[HTML]{95CCDD}{\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{n}}} & {\bfseries \cellcolor[HTML]{95CCDD}{\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{Overall}}} & {\bfseries \cellcolor[HTML]{95CCDD}{\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{Female}}} & {\bfseries \cellcolor[HTML]{95CCDD}{\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{Male}}} & {\bfseries \cellcolor[HTML]{95CCDD}{\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{Statistic}}} & {\bfseries \cellcolor[HTML]{95CCDD}{\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{p}}} \\

{\itshape \textcolor[HTML]{293681}{age}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{300}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{46.76 ± 14.15}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{46.42 ± 13.63}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{47.35 ± 15.09}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{t = -0.53}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{5.992006e-01}} \\ 
{\itshape \textcolor[HTML]{293681}{hdl}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{300}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{46.81 ± 10.11}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{48.32 ± 9.78}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{44.08 ± 10.18}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{t = 3.50}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{5.599878e-04}} \\

{\itshape \textcolor[HTML]{293681}{verbal\_fluency}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{300}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{14.98 ± 4.63}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{15.08 ± 4.44}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{14.79 ± 4.97}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{t = 0.52}} & {\textcolor[HTML]{293681}{6.062013e-01}} \\

what: Render a clerk_tbl as an interactive HTML table — render_reactable
title: Render a clerk_tbl as an interactive HTML table — render_reactable
text: Renders clerk_tbl reactable interactive HTML table. Typically called indirectly via clerk_render().

title: Render a clerk_tbl as an interactive HTML table — render_reactable
code: render_reactable(x, title = NULL, ...)

title: Render a clerk_tbl as an interactive HTML table — render_reactable
text: x clerk_tbl object. title Optional character string displayed table. ... Passed reactable::reactable().

title: Render a clerk_tbl as an interactive HTML table — render_reactable
text: reactable htmlwidget.

title: Render a clerk_tbl as an interactive HTML table — render_reactable
Correlation / partial correlation table — tbl_correlation
text: Formats tidy data frame (partial) correlation results publication-ready table. Expects pre-computed coefficients tidy data frame one row per predictor x outcome pair. Column-name arguments accept character strings (quoted names). Defaults match typical correlation results frame columns named variable, outcome, r, p.

title: Correlation / partial correlation table — tbl_correlation
code: tbl_correlation(   data,   predictor = "variable",   outcome = "outcome",   r = "r",   p = "p",   n = NULL,   extra_cols = NULL,   domains = list(),   fdr = FALSE,   fdr_within = NULL,   digits = 3,   p_digits = 3,   pivot = FALSE,   output = c("gt", "html", "latex") )

title: Correlation / partial correlation table — tbl_correlation
text: data tidy data frame correlation results. predictor Character string. Name predictor variable column. Default "variable". outcome Character string. Name outcome variable column. Default "outcome". r Character string. Name correlation coefficient column. Default "r". p Character string. Name p-value column. Default "p". n Character string NULL. Name sample size column. Default NULL (omitted). extra_cols Character vector additional column names carry (e.g. "hemisphere", "lobe"). domains named list mapping predictor variable names domain/section labels. fdr Logical. Apply BH FDR correction (default FALSE). fdr_within Character string NULL. Column name group FDR correction within (e.g. "outcome"). digits Integer. Decimal places r (default 3). p_digits Integer. Decimal places p-values (default 3). pivot Logical. Pivot wide format one column per outcome (default FALSE). output Character string. One "gt" (default), "html", "latex".

title: Correlation / partial correlation table — tbl_correlation
text: clerk_tbl object type "correlation".

title: Correlation / partial correlation table — tbl_correlation
code: tbl_correlation(   clerk_cor_example,   domains = list(     "Metabolic"      = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Anthropometric" = c("waist", "systolic_bp"),     "Mental health"  = c("bdi", "panas_neg")   ),   fdr    = TRUE,   output = "gt" ) |> clerk_render(title = "Partial correlations (age + sex controlled)") #> Warning: invalid factor level, NA generated     Partial correlations (age + sex controlled)            Outcome       r       p       p (FDR)     Metabolic     hdl tmt_time +0.190 0.008 0.032glucose tmt_time -0.229 0.398 0.398bmi tmt_time -0.086 0.013 0.042hdl verbal_fluency +0.078 0.072 0.154glucose verbal_fluency +0.216 0.003 0.024bmi verbal_fluency +0.022 0.207 0.255Anthropometric     waist tmt_time +0.279 0.002 0.024systolic_bp tmt_time +0.195 0.379 0.398waist verbal_fluency +0.047 0.185 0.247systolic_bp verbal_fluency -0.105 0.077 0.154Mental health     bdi tmt_time +0.096 0.109 0.170panas_neg tmt_time +0.157 0.006 0.032bdi verbal_fluency -0.176 0.070 0.154panas_neg verbal_fluency +0.232 0.117 0.170NA     life_satisfaction tmt_time +0.247 0.117 0.170life_satisfaction verbal_fluency +0.201 0.234 0.267

what: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
title: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
text: Produces descriptive/Table 1-style summary data frame, optional group comparisons. Continuous variables summarised mean ± SD compared independent-samples t-test (two groups) one-way ANOVA (three groups). Categorical variables summarised n (%) compared chi-squared test.

title: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
code: tbl_descriptive(   data,   group = NULL,   vars = NULL,   domains = list(),   log_vars = character(0),   digits = 2,   p_digits = 3,   fdr = FALSE,   overall = TRUE,   output = c("gt", "html", "latex") )

title: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
text: data data frame. group <tidy-select> Unquoted name grouping variable. NULL (default) overall sample summarised without comparison column. vars <tidy-select> Variables include. Defaults columns except group. domains named list mapping variable names domain/section labels, e.g. list("Metabolic" = c("hdl", "glucose"), "Anthropometric" = c("bmi", "waist")). Variables mentioned placed "" section. log_vars Character vector variable names log-transformed prior analysis. footnote appended noting values shown raw scale. digits Integer. Number decimal places continuous variables (default 2). p_digits Integer. Number decimal places p-values (default 3). fdr Logical. Apply BH FDR correction p-values across tests (default FALSE). overall Logical. Include overall (ungrouped) column alongside group columns (default TRUE). output Character string specifying render target. One "gt" (default, Word/PDF via gt), "html" (interactive reactable), "latex" (LaTeX via gt::as_latex()). value stored returned object used clerk_render() dispatch correct renderer automatically.

title: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
text: clerk_tbl object (list class "clerk_tbl") containing: table data frame one row per variable. domains domain list supplied user. log_vars log-transformed variable names. type Character string "descriptive". group Name grouping variable, NULL. output render target: "gt", "html", "latex".

title: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
code: tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group    = sex,   domains  = list(     "Metabolic"    = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Cognitive"    = c("tmt_time", "verbal_fluency"),     "Mental health"= c("bdi", "panas_neg")   ),   log_vars = "tmt_time",   output   = "gt" ) |> clerk_render(title = "Table 1. Sample characteristics by sex") #> Warning: invalid factor level, NA generated     Table 1. Sample characteristics by sex            n       Overall       Female       Male       Statistic       p     NA     age 300 46.76 ± 14.15 46.42 ± 13.63 47.35 ± 15.09 t = -0.53 5.992006e-01waist 300 92.07 ± 11.56 92.14 ± 11.32 91.93 ± 12.03 t = 0.15 8.792922e-01systolic_bp 300 123.25 ± 17.77 120.36 ± 17.86 128.48 ± 16.45 t = -3.97 9.521407e-05life_satisfaction 300 18.02 ± 4.23 17.40 ± 4.25 19.14 ± 3.99 t = -3.54 4.880922e-04Metabolic     hdl 300 46.81 ± 10.11 48.32 ± 9.78 44.08 ± 10.18 t = 3.50 5.599878e-04glucose 300 91.68 ± 24.65 92.61 ± 24.49 90.01 ± 24.96 t = 0.87 3.863309e-01bmi 300 26.15 ± 4.91 26.43 ± 4.84 25.64 ± 5.01 t = 1.32 1.895409e-01Cognitive     tmt_time1 300 129.24 ± 64.98 132.81 ± 66.77 122.81 ± 61.40 t = 1.31 1.916489e-01verbal_fluency 300 14.98 ± 4.63 15.08 ± 4.44 14.79 ± 4.97 t = 0.52 6.062013e-01Mental health     bdi 300 13.64 ± 9.26 15.07 ± 8.97 11.05 ± 9.26 t = 3.65 3.325514e-04panas_neg 300 20.98 ± 7.48 22.50 ± 7.27 18.25 ± 7.09 t = 4.92 1.662527e-061 Log-transformed variables shown on raw scale.

what: Heritability and variance components table — tbl_heritability
title: Heritability and variance components table — tbl_heritability
text: Formats tidy data frame narrow-sense heritability estimates (h2) publication-ready table. Column-name defaults match output R-itable::herit_batch() directly. Column-name arguments accept character strings (quoted names).

title: Heritability and variance components table — tbl_heritability
code: tbl_heritability(   data,   metric = "trait",   h2 = "h2",   ci_low = "ci_lo",   ci_high = "ci_hi",   p = "pval",   sigma2_a = NULL,   sigma2_e = NULL,   model = NULL,   domains = list(),   fdr = FALSE,   fdr_within = NULL,   digits = 2,   p_digits = 3,   output = c("gt", "html", "latex") )

title: Heritability and variance components table — tbl_heritability
text: data tidy data frame one row per trait x covariate model. metric Character string. Name trait/phenotype column. Default "trait" (matches herit_batch()). h2 Character string. Name h2 estimate column. Default "h2". ci_low Character string. Name lower 95% CI bound column. Default "ci_lo" (matches herit_batch()). ci_high Character string. Name upper 95% CI bound column. Default "ci_hi" (matches herit_batch()). p Character string. Name one-sided LRT p-value column. Default "pval" (matches herit_batch()). sigma2_a Character string NULL. Name additive genetic variance column. Default NULL. sigma2_e Character string NULL. Name residual variance column. Default NULL. model Character string NULL. Name column identifying covariate models. Pass "covariates" using herit_batch() output. Default NULL. domains named list mapping trait names domain/section labels. fdr Logical. Apply BH FDR correction (default FALSE). fdr_within Character string NULL. Column name group FDR correction within. digits Integer. Decimal places h2 variance components (default 2). p_digits Integer. Decimal places p-values (default 3). output Character string. One "gt" (default), "html", "latex".

title: Heritability and variance components table — tbl_heritability
text: clerk_tbl object type "heritability".

title: Heritability and variance components table — tbl_heritability

title: Regression coefficients table — tbl_regression
text: Formats tidy data frame regression results publication-ready table. Designed accept output broom::tidy() directly. Column-name arguments accept character strings (quoted names). Defaults match broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) output.

title: Regression coefficients table — tbl_regression
code: tbl_regression(   data,   term = "term",   estimate = "estimate",   std_error = "std.error",   conf_low = "conf.low",   conf_high = "conf.high",   p = "p.value",   model = NULL,   domains = list(),   exponentiate = FALSE,   fdr = FALSE,   fdr_within = NULL,   ci_sep = ", ",   digits = 3,   p_digits = 3,   output = c("gt", "html", "latex") )

title: Regression coefficients table — tbl_regression
text: data tidy data frame regression results one row per term. term Character string. Name model term column. Default "term". estimate Character string. Name coefficient column. Default "estimate". std_error Character string. Name standard error column. Default "std.error". conf_low Character string. Name lower CI bound column. Default "conf.low". conf_high Character string. Name upper CI bound column. Default "conf.high". p Character string. Name p-value column. Default "p.value". model Character string NULL. Name column identifying multiple models. Default NULL. domains named list mapping term names domain/section labels. exponentiate Logical. Exponentiate estimates CIs (default FALSE). fdr Logical. Apply BH FDR correction (default FALSE). fdr_within Character string NULL. Column name group FDR correction within. ci_sep Character string separating CI bounds (default ", "). digits Integer. Decimal places estimates (default 3). p_digits Integer. Decimal places p-values (default 3). output Character string. One "gt" (default), "html", "latex".

title: Regression coefficients table — tbl_regression
text: clerk_tbl object type "regression".

title: Regression coefficients table — tbl_regression

what: Simple descriptive summary table (no inferential tests) — tbl_simple
title: Simple descriptive summary table (no inferential tests) — tbl_simple
text: Produces concise descriptive summary data frame — mean ± SD continuous variables n (%) categorical variables — group comparisons statistical tests.

title: Simple descriptive summary table (no inferential tests) — tbl_simple
code: tbl_simple(   data,   vars = NULL,   domains = list(),   log_vars = character(0),   digits = 2,   output = c("gt", "html", "latex") )

title: Simple descriptive summary table (no inferential tests) — tbl_simple
text: data data frame. vars <tidy-select> Variables include. Defaults columns. domains named list mapping variable names domain/section labels. log_vars Character vector log-transformed variable names. digits Integer. Decimal places continuous variables (default 2). output Character string. One "gt" (default), "html", "latex".

title: Simple descriptive summary table (no inferential tests) — tbl_simple
text: clerk_tbl object type "simple".

title: Simple descriptive summary table (no inferential tests) — tbl_simple
